mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production above its 12-month moving average signals strengthening economic output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansions above the 12-month trend yield negative 5-day returns in > 60% of cases during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low federal funds rates combined with rising unemployment indicate a liquidity trap where monetary easing fails to stimulate growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-UNRATE interaction shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) during low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 58% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS readings above 200,000 fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases with accuracy below 50%.
